---
title: "About"
---

Economic Snapshot brings together core U.S. economic indicators — labor,
consumers, travel, public health, and financial markets — in one place, pulled
from live public sources through APIs and web scrapers and rendered as
interactive charts.

Economic Snapshot was developed by Faelynn Carroll, Ethan Kapstein, and Jacob
N. Shapiro of Princeton University.

## Data sources

- **FRED** (Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis) — unemployment, payrolls,
  initial and continued claims, job openings, wage growth, consumer prices,
  rent, gasoline, the 30-year mortgage rate, sentiment, consumer loans,
  durable goods, vehicle sales, Treasury yields, the S&P 500, the dollar
  index, the GDP nowcast, housing starts, and airline load factor.
- **BLS** (Bureau of Labor Statistics) — civilian labor force by state.
- **TSA** — daily checkpoint passenger volumes.
- **CDC** — confirmed measles cases by week of rash onset.
- **International Trade Administration (I-94)** — monthly international visitor
  arrivals by country of origin.

## Methods

Data are visualized with no or minimal pre-processing and use the highest
available frequencies so the charts reflect the maximum amount of information.
Where a series is published more often than it reads clearly — daily market
data, weekly state claims — it is averaged up to a weekly or monthly value;
the level of aggregation is stated in the source line beneath each chart.

<!-- Faelynn to add further methodology notes here. -->


{{< include _footer.qmd >}}
